title: What's an application/octet-stream file?
content:
Hello,

I've had this problem with viewing expanded jpg's that up until now, I've been trying to get along with. On the website, milesplit.com, there's an option at the bottom of every picture, it gives the option saying to click here to view a larger version. After I click it, I get a small box saying "You have chosen to open..." and then a big string of letters and numbers for the file name. It continues "which is: application/octet-stream file (156 KB) from: d193frjqb908ar.cloudfront.net" And then asks what Firefox should do with it, either open or save it. From there, I always select Open with and click on Browse. The next screen is long and narrow and at the top it says to Choose Helper App. Then, it gives the application/octet-stream name, which happens to be the same no matter what picture you're looking at, and then it says Send this item to and gives (in my case) 6 apps to readily click on, with Firefox being at the top. As I always want Firefox to open it, I just click OK and then click OK again in the smaller box. And finally, the larger version of the picture is displayed in another tab. Now, I've been using this site on and off for at least 15 years and before a few years ago, I had no problems. So, is this a fixable problem, or do I have to keep on doing what I have been doing?

title: html text box content not showing in print preview or print after updating to 150
content:
Hi all, I am having weird issue and I don't know if this is some kind of new feature or bug.

We use own web server and our self made erp system which runs using php and mysql.

We have web forms that contains customer and product information. We can print those forms and they are slight different designed for viewing and for printing.

Now, after updating to 150.0 Firefox, some text boxes does not show content in print preview or printing. If, for example, I have text box in form, that has customer name inside, print preview shows text box but not customer name inside it. This seems to happen only if there is "media print" css rules applied.

We have those rules in css file: " @media print 

   .no-print, .no-print *    {        display: none !important;    }

.only-print, .only-print * 

   {    display:enabled !important;    }

" Now, if web page uses "only-print" div, firefox prints text and other objects ok, but not content of text boxes. Something has been changes as this has been working so far many years, and also works with other browsers. I have no clue why content is not printing. If there is no "only-print" div, everything prints ok. Our forms include a lot of this kind of formatting, so it is almost impossible to recode all.

This is example how text box is coded in php: "

echo "
Customer number: <input type=\"text\" size=\"7\" name=\"id\" value=\"$cust_num\" >
";

"

In this case above text "Customer number:" prints ok and also input box but variable $cust_num inside it does not. It does not matter if I put plain text in place of variable. So input text box content basically is not printing.

I have tried resetting firefox, running it in safe mode and in private window. Nothing helps. Tried android firefox also and it has same behavior. Versions before 150 works ok.

Can somebody light me up what has changed?
creator: ismo56
